mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and raise equity discount rates, directly suppressing valuations and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate hikes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, it signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE remains below 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in CPIAUCSL and increases in retail sales indicate falling inflation alongside robust consumer demand, which expands corporate profit margins and drives positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to outperform baseline in >60% of overlapping 60-day windows, or correlation between proxy divergence and forward returns drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, a monthly increase in the industrial production index reflects rapid capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, generating positive short-term market momentum.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >50% of months with rising INDPRO during recovery periods, or rolling IC falls below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above trend levels signals expanding economic activity that supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with GDPC1 growth rates, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 320.0, it triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and yields negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Market returns remain positive in >65% of periods where CPIAUCSL crosses the threshold, or the signal fails to predict direction in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent rise in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market strength, boosting consumer confidence and generating positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal produces negative 20-day returns in >55% of instances, or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During housing market correction periods, a sustained drop in housing starts signals weakening construction activity and reduced household wealth effects, leading to negative short-term market returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of months with declining HOUST during correction regimes, or the signal shows zero predictive IC.
